page: Microsoft Azure E2_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from a single physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng falls in the average range at 1405.69, while multi-core performance is constrained, scoring 1845.28. Memory bandwidth is relatively low, with read-only large block transfers at 8.21 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 7.37 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tasks show weak to poor performance, with a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 23.7 GB/sec and a Passmark CPU encryption test score of 846.72.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, the E2_v4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint without demanding high compute density. However, its poor multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth make it less suitable for intensive parallel processing, large-scale database operations, or complex LLM inference. It is best suited for lightweight memory-bound applications, basic caching layers, or small-scale web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over raw processing throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure E2_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance but poor multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds at 8.21 GB/sec. This profile makes the E2_v4 a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-optimized workloads that do not require intensive parallel computation, such as lightweight caching, small databases, or memory-bound utility applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, optimized for lightweight memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-optimized workloads.
tagline: An x86 virtual machine offering a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight, memory-bound applications without local storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Average single-core performance with limited multi-core scaling
Best suited for lightweight memory-bound workloads and caching
